introSection: Blue Streak is a wooden out and back roller coaster located at Cedar Point in Sandusky, Ohio, USA. Opened in 1964, it is the oldest operating roller coaster at the park.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
Blue Streak opened on May 23, 1964. At first, it was unpainted. By 1967, it had received its current blue paint.

On April 26, 2013, Blue Streak was damaged when a construction vehicle backed into its turnaround. No major damage occurred and repairs were carried out quickly.

During the 2018 Halloweekend's event, Blue Streak was named "Boo Streak". The ride's signage and station music was changed to reflect this.

On June 20, 2022, American Coaster Enthusiasts recognized the ride as an ACE Roller Coaster Landmark during Coaster Con 44.

sections.title: Incident
sections.content: On August 24, 1980, five riders suffered minor injuries when two trains collided. The park said the mishap occurred when an irate customer interfered with the operators of the ride, allowing an incoming train to collide with an outgoing train.

sections.title: Name
sections.content: The ride was named after "The Blue Streaks", the nickname of the Sandusky High School athletic teams.

fullText:
Blue Streak is a wooden out and back roller coaster located at Cedar Point in Sandusky, Ohio, USA. Opened in 1964, it is the oldest operating roller coaster at the park.

Blue Streak opened on May 23, 1964. At first, it was unpainted. By 1967, it had received its current blue paint.

On April 26, 2013, Blue Streak was damaged when a construction vehicle backed into its turnaround. No major damage occurred and repairs were carried out quickly.

During the 2018 Halloweekend's event, Blue Streak was named "Boo Streak". The ride's signage and station music was changed to reflect this.

On June 20, 2022, American Coaster Enthusiasts recognized the ride as an ACE Roller Coaster Landmark during Coaster Con 44.



Blue track and white supports.



On August 24, 1980, five riders suffered minor injuries when two trains collided. The park said the mishap occurred when an irate customer interfered with the operators of the ride, allowing an incoming train to collide with an outgoing train.

The ride was named after "The Blue Streaks", the nickname of the Sandusky High School athletic teams.
